hypothesis: In property-driven generative optimization tasks sensitive to 3D molecular features (e.g., singlet/triplet excited-state energies), integrating 3D-conformation-aware diversity metrics (e.g., distributions of lowest-energy conformer RMSDs and 3D fragment-shape descriptors) into NaviDiv’s adaptive constraint module will preserve a higher fraction of functionally relevant diversity—measured as a statistically larger variance in predicted S1 and T1 energies among generated molecules at convergence—while requiring equal or fewer reinforcement-learning iterations to reach the same average property score compared to using combined 1D/2D (fingerprint, SMILES, fragment) constraints alone.
